sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Stade Rennais, currently sitting at 11th in the league with 38 points, host Nice, who are in an impressive 4th place with 57 points, at Roazhon Park in Rennes. This matchup promises to be intriguing as Nice come into the game with a stronger league standing. The referee for this encounter, Jeremy Stinat, averages 3.5 yellow cards per game, which could influence the physicality of the match. With Nice on a streak of 3 wins and unbeaten in their last 4 games, they appear to be the favorites on paper. Meanwhile, Stade Rennais have struggled defensively, failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 5 matches.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Nice have had the upper hand with 3 wins compared to Stade Rennais' 2, with no draws recorded. Their most recent clash on January 3, 2025, saw Nice edge out Stade Rennais 3-2 at home, with Evann Guessand earning Player of the Match honors with an 8.1 Sofascore rating. Head-to-head trends show both teams scoring in 6 out of their last 8 encounters, suggesting we could see goals at both ends. Additionally, Nice have been the first to score in 6 of those 8 games, indicating their ability to start strong against Rennais.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Stade Rennais
sections.text: For Stade Rennais, Arnaud Kalimuendo stands out as a major threat up front, ranking 4th in the league for goals scored with 15, surpassing his expected goals of 14.15. Ludovic Blas is another key figure, contributing 6 goals and 7 assists with a strong average Sofascore rating of 7.22. Defensively, Lilian Brassier impresses with a league rank of 5 for aerial duels won at 75.4%, which could be crucial against Nice's attack. In-form player Adrien Truffert, with a recent average rating of 7.68 over the last 5 matches, has also chipped in with 1 goal and 2 assists. However, the absence of defender Alidu Seidu, who ranks 2nd in the league for ground duels won at 71.6% and tops the chart for times fouled per game at 2.5, might weaken Rennais’ backline.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Nice
sections.text: Nice boast firepower with Evann Guessand leading the line, having scored 11 goals and provided 7 assists while overperforming his expected goals of 8.13 by 2.87. Jonathan Clauss is a standout in midfield, ranking high with 8 assists and an average Sofascore rating of 7.4, and he’s in form with 3 assists in his last 5 games. Gaëtan Laborde adds depth with 9 goals, though he has slightly underperformed his expected goals of 10.2. Defender Tom Louchet also catches the eye, ranking 2nd in the league for aerial duels won at 78.6%. However, Nice will miss key players like Youssouf Ndayishimiye, who has scored 3 goals from defense, potentially impacting their solidity.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Team Stats
sections.text: Stade Rennais have scored 47 goals this season, ranking 9th in the league, and excel in aerial duels with a 53.1% win rate, placing them 4th. They’ve also created 65 big chances but missed 39, though their conversion rate of 40% ranks 4th. Defensively, they’ve conceded 46 goals, ranking 11th for least goals conceded, and haven’t kept a clean sheet in 5 games. Nice, meanwhile, show strength in goal distribution, with 13 goals each in the 16-30 and 31-45 minute periods, indicating early dominance. Their streak of scoring first in 6 of their last 7 games, and winning the first half in 5 of those, suggests they could take an early lead.

sections.subtitle: Historical Standout and Match Trends
sections.text: Brice Samba of Nice has been a standout in the last 5 matchups against Stade Rennais, averaging a Sofascore rating of 7.32 and making 3.4 saves per game across 5 starts. Matches between these sides often see over 2.5 goals, with 5 out of the last 6 exceeding this mark, pointing to a potentially high-scoring affair. Both teams have scored in 6 of Nice’s last 7 away games and 4 of Rennais’ last 5 home games, reinforcing the likelihood of goals on both sides. Card-wise, their head-to-head games have seen less than 4.5 cards in 5 of the last 6 encounters, aligning with Nice’s recent trend of low card counts in 5 of 5 away matches.

sections.subtitle: Venue and Atmosphere
sections.text: Roazhon Park in Rennes, the home ground of Stade Rennais, will host this clash, potentially giving the home side a morale boost despite their recent defensive woes. Rennais average 5.125 corners per game, ranking 5th in the league, which could lead to set-piece opportunities in front of their home crowd. With 51.5% average ball possession (7th in the league), they might look to control the tempo at home. However, Nice’s ability to win the first half in 5 of their last 7 away games could test the home fans’ patience early on. The atmosphere is expected to be electric as Rennais aim to climb the table against a top-4 side.
